text: AI is increasingly common in the workplace, changing how we work in several ways. Many routine, repetitive tasks can now be handled through automation, allowing employees to focus on more complex and creative work. Productivity tools powered by AI help people analyse data quickly, manage schedules, and even draft documents. This means workers can achieve more in less time. However, AI doesn't usually replace jobs entirely; instead, it encourages human-AI collaboration. People work alongside AI systems, using their judgment to check AI's suggestions and make final decisions. As a result, many jobs are evolving. Workers may need to learn new digital skills to use these tools effectively, making continuous learning important in today's workplace.
